output.hypothesis: With launch shape, iterations, and controls held fixed, the missing 256 KB, 4-byte-stride cell will measure higher estimated bandwidth than the accepted 256 KB, 128-byte-stride result; if it does not, the hostile-stride penalty does not persist clearly at the reduced working set under this probe.
output.rationale: Complete the minimal 2x2 matrix for axes `working_set_kb` in {2048, 256} and `stride_bytes` in {128, 4} using the real CUDA probe. Already measured cells are spec_3626106571cf (2048 KB, 128 B), spec_ea7294e5fd1a (2048 KB, 4 B), and spec_d89a9f30c9b4 (256 KB, 128 B). This spec fills the single missing matched-control cell (256 KB, 4 B) while preserving single-GPU scope, deterministic controls, benchmark family, and the 120-second round budget.
output.selected_question: Does the missing matched-control cell at 256 KB working set and 4-byte stride show substantially higher effective bandwidth than the 256 KB, 128-byte hostile-stride case under the same CUDA memory probe launch parameters?
